task: I’m setting up a new kitchen and want one of the first things I make to be roasted Brussels sprouts, so could you start on Google and find me a recipe that clearly uses both Parmesan and balsamic vinegar, then open the actual recipe page and note the title, oven temperature, and cook time because I want to make sure the cookware I buy fits that kind of roasting setup. Once you’ve got that recipe open, head to Le Creuset and look for a light green Dutch oven, and specifically check whether the 5.5 qt size is offered in that color so I can see if it would work for recipes in that range; please open the product page itself and leave it open so I can look at the color and size options on the page. While you’re at it, I’m also sorting out kitchen appliances before I start cooking, so go to YouTube and find a practical video about whether a dishwasher installation needs an air gap, open the video page, start playing it, and tell me the main decision points like when an air gap is required, when a high loop is used instead, and why the air gap exists in the first place. Please keep the recipe tab, the Le Creuset product tab, and the YouTube video tab open in separate tabs so I can compare everything visually afterward.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for and opened a recipe for "Roasted Brussels Sprouts with Parmesan" (Step 6). The recipe page (allrecipes.com/recipe/276723/roasted-brussels-sprouts-with-parmesan/) is a standard recipe page that includes the title, oven temperature (400°F), and cook time (25 minutes). The agent confirmed in its final summary that the recipe uses both Parmesan and balsamic vinegar, which is consistent with the recipe title and typical content for this specific Allrecipes page. The rubric requirement is satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Le Creuset website, located the "Signature Round Dutch Oven" product page, and selected the "Sea Salt" color (which is the light green color offered by Le Creuset). The agent confirmed in its final summary that the 5.5 qt size is available in this color and is in stock. The screenshots and action history confirm the agent interacted with the product page and verified the specific color and size requirements.

R3: success - The agent successfully opened a relevant YouTube video ("Dishwasher Air Gap-Do You Need One?" by BobsPlumbingVideos) and confirmed it was playing. The agent provided a summary that correctly identifies:
1. When an air gap is required (local plumbing code).
2. The high loop alternative (looping the drain hose high under the counter).
3. Why an air gap exists (to prevent backflow of dirty water into the dishwasher and provide a physical break).
The agent maintained the required tabs throughout the process.

R4: success - The agent was tasked with keeping three specific pages open in separate tabs: a recipe page, a Le Creuset product page, and a YouTube video page. 

Reviewing the action history:
- Step 3: Opened Google (Tab 0).
- Step 6: Navigated to the recipe page (Tab 0).
- Step 9: Opened Le Creuset (Tab 1).
- Step 20: Opened the YouTube video (Tab 2).

The agent explicitly used `openTab` to manage these separate tabs. The final response confirms that the agent "left all three tabs open separately." While the provided screenshots only show the final state of the last tab (YouTube), the action history confirms the creation and navigation of three distinct tabs (tabs[0], tabs[1], tabs[2]). The agent successfully maintained the browser state as requested.
